macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: One Year Since Trump's 'Liberation Day' Tariffs: Markets React to April 2025 Announcement Impact
items.summary: On the one-year anniversary of President Trump's sweeping April 2, 2025 tariff announcement imposing 10% tariffs on nearly every country and up to 50% on select nations, Financial Times and trade experts analyze the market fallout, trading partner retaliation, and ongoing negotiations including potential US-Canada-Mexico renegotiations expected to close in summer 2026.

items.title: Stock Market Vulnerability Amid Consumer Spending Concerns Following Iran War
items.summary: Morgan Stanley's Chief Economic Strategist warns recession probability is higher than expected, citing stock market vulnerability as a key risk factor. Corporate equities now account for over 25% of household net worth at record 80-year highs, making consumers vulnerable to equity corrections that could sharply reduce spending from higher-income households.

items.title: S&P 500 Down 5% for March Despite Tuesday Rally on Iran Conflict Resolution Optimism
items.summary: The S&P 500 experienced its worst monthly performance in a year during March 2026, declining 5% despite a Tuesday surge on optimism about potential US-Israeli-Iran conflict resolution. Household savings rates have declined to 4.5% from 5.1% a year prior, reducing consumer financial cushion.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $216M in 24-Hour Volatility
items.summary: Global cryptocurrency markets saw over $216 million in futures liquidations, led by Ethereum ($96.57M), Bitcoin ($95.71M), and Solana ($24.58M), amid significant market shakeup.

items.title: Ethereum Drops 4% to $2,046, Outpacing Bitcoin's 3% Decline
items.summary: Ethereum fell 4.07% to $2,046 in the past 24 hours, worse than Bitcoin's 3.11% drop to $66,473, with Solana down 5.62%, linked to Trump's Iran escalation signals.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips 3% to $67,000 on Trump's Military Escalation Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin declined nearly 3% in 24 hours to around $67,000 as market sentiment soured from U.S. President Trump's signals of continued military action.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Hits 2026 Low for Key Buyers Amid 2.8% Daily Drop
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $67,000, down 2.8% in 24 hours and nearly 23% year-to-date, as a major buyer group reached its 2026 low.

items.title: Crypto Market Declines on Middle East Tensions and ETF Outflows
items.summary: The broader crypto market dropped today due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and outflows from cryptocurrency Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs).

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Yann LeCun Discusses New AI Approach Beyond LLMs at Brown University
items.summary: AI pioneer Yann LeCun argued in a Brown University lecture on April 1 that large language models are not the path to human-level intelligence and advocated for systems that build abstract world models to predict action outcomes safely. His new company, AMI Labs, raised over $1 billion for this world model approach.

items.title: MIT Researchers Develop SEED-SET Framework for Ethical AI Testing in Autonomous Systems
items.summary: MIT introduced SEED-SET, a testing framework that generates scenarios to evaluate fairness in AI decision-support systems like power grids and traffic routing, uncovering ethical issues more effectively than baselines. The research, to be presented at ICLR, was tested on realistic systems and responds to user preferences.

items.title: Anthropic Model Miscue Highlights Tensions Between AI Developers and Cybersecurity Firms
items.summary: A recent Anthropic model advancement has intensified conflicts between AI companies and cybersecurity vendors, as rapid capability leaps challenge security measures.

items.title: University of Manchester GeoAI Study Reveals Complex England Productivity Patterns
items.summary: Using GeoAI, researchers found England's productivity divide is more nuanced than a North-South split, with factors like info-comms jobs, wages, and proximity to high-productivity areas key; nearly half of local authorities lag in both level and growth from 2010-2022.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Myrient Gaming Preservation Site Shut Down, Revived as Minerva Archive
items.summary: Popular video game preservation archive Myrient shut down but has been fully backed up and relaunched as the Minerva Archive project, ensuring rare retro games are preserved.

items.title: Crimson Desert Faces Criticism from Larian Studios Amid Sales Milestone
items.summary: Crimson Desert received criticism from Larian Studios (Baldur's Gate 3 developers) in a recent update, while celebrating 4 million copies sold.

items.title: Upcoming PC Games and Updates for April 2026 Announced
items.summary: Key releases include Road to Vostok (April 7), Lay of the Land (April 8), Pragmata (April 17), Masters of Albion (April 22), and Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred (April 28), plus updates like Starfield Free Lanes.

items.title: PS5 Pro PSSR 2.0 Visual Upgrade Showcased in The Last of Us Part I
items.summary: New PSSR 2.0 technology on PS5 Pro delivers enhanced visuals in The Last of Us Part I, highlighted in a recent showcase video.

items.title: RAID: Shadow Legends Brings Back Champion Skins and April Fools Content
items.summary: Plarium announces return of champion skins after a long absence, plus new April Fools avatar and other in-game updates like Bayek last chance event.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NASA Launches Artemis II Mission on First Crewed Lunar Flyby in 50 Years
items.summary: NASA successfully launched four astronauts aboard a massive rocket for the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby since over 50 years ago. The mission blasted off on Wednesday, initiating a long-anticipated journey around the moon.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II Achieves Liftoff for Historic Moon Mission
items.summary: Four astronauts have been sent around the moon for the first time in 50 years via NASA's Artemis II launch on April 1, 2026. This crewed mission represents a major milestone in space exploration.
